Title: Product Variations - Size Selection
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
Product with size variations (S/M/L/XL)
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to product with size variations (e.g., T-Shirt)
2. [ASSERT] Verify size selector displays
3. [ASSERT] Verify all sizes listed (S, M, L, XL)
4. [ACT] Click size 'M'
5. [ASSERT] Verify size M is selected (highlighted)
6. [ASSERT] Verify Add to Cart is enabled
7. [ACT] Click Add to Cart
8. [NAV] Navigate to cart
9. [ASSERT] Verify selected size shown in cart
Assertions:
[DOM] Size selector element is visible
[STATE] Size 'M' is selected with visual highlight
[STATE] Add to Cart button is enabled
[DOM] Cart line displays: Product Name - Size: M
[DOM] Correct SKU for selected size variant shown
[FORMAT] Price correct for selected size variant
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] mutation errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Size selector does not display; incorrect size shown in cart; Add to Cart not enabled after size selection; cart shows wrong SKU

Title: Product Configuration - Price Calculation Accuracy
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
Configurable product with multiple price modifiers
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to configurable product
2. [ASSERT] Note base price: $100
3. [ACT] Select option with +$50 modifier
4. [ASSERT] Verify total: $150
5. [ACT] Select option with +$25 modifier
6. [ASSERT] Verify total: $175
7. [ACT] Select option with -$10 discount
8. [ASSERT] Verify total: $165
9. [ACT] Add to cart
10. [ASSERT] Verify cart price: $165
11. [NAV] Proceed to checkout
12. [ASSERT] Verify order total uses $165
Assertions:
[MATH] Running total updates accurately with each modifier applied: $100 + $50 + $25 - $10 = $165
[DOM] Each option shows price modifier: +$50, +$25, -$10
[MATH] Cart price matches configured price: $165
[MATH] Checkout and order confirmation use $165 (no price discrepancies)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] mutation errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Rounding errors in price calculation; cart price differs from configured price; checkout uses different price than cart

Title: Configurable Product - Discount Scales Correctly with Quantity
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
Configurable product with discounted options, quantity > 1
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to configurable product page (e.g., bike builder)
2. [ACT] Select a configuration option that has a discount (list price > sale price)
3. [ACT] Set quantity to 2 or more
4. [ASSERT] Observe the subtotal in the configuration section
5. [ASSERT] Verify cart subtotal = salePrice x quantity
6. [ASSERT] Verify cart discount amount = (listPrice - salePrice) x quantity
Assertions:
[MATH] Subtotal = salePrice x quantity (NOT listPrice x quantity)
[MATH] Cart discount amount = (listPrice - salePrice) x quantity
[MATH] Cart subtotal is mathematically correct with no rounding errors
[FORMAT] No pricing discrepancy between product page and cart
[DOM] VCST-4586 regression: sale price used for all quantity calculations
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] mutation errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Subtotal uses listPrice instead of salePrice; discount amount not scaling with quantity; pricing discrepancy between product page and cart

Title: Configurable Product - Sale Price Displayed as Primary Price

[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]

2. [ACT] Select an option with a discount applied (list price > sale price)
3. [ASSERT] Observe price display in configuration section
4. [ASSERT] Verify which price is shown prominently
Assertions:
[DOM] Sale price (salePrice) displayed prominently (bold, dark color)
[DOM] List price (listPrice) shown with strikethrough formatting
[FORMAT] Visual presentation: salePrice = primary; listPrice = secondary or crossed out
[FORMAT] Example: $88.00 sale shown large; $126.00 crossed out shown smaller
[DOM] VCST-4586 regression: sale price must be primary
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: List price shown as primary price; sale price shown with strikethrough (reversed display); no strikethrough on list price

Title: Configurable Product - No Auto-Scroll on First Collapsed Section Click
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
Fresh page load of configurable product with multiple accordion sections (some collapsed by default)
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to configurable product page (fresh load - no cached state)
2. [WAIT] Wait for page to fully load
3. [ASSERT] Locate a configuration accordion section that is COLLAPSED by default
4. [ACT] Click the accordion header to expand it
5. [ACT] Immediately click a radio button option inside the newly expanded section
6. [ASSERT] Verify page scroll position has not changed
Assertions:
[STATE] Page does NOT auto-scroll after first radio button click
[STATE] Radio button selection STICKS (does not revert to 'None' or default)
[NAV] Scroll position remains where user was before click
[STATE] Selected option confirmed as checked after 1-2 seconds
[DOM] VCST-4612 regression case: no auto-scroll on first click
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Page auto-scrolls after clicking radio button in newly expanded section; radio button selection reverts to None after first click

Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Radio button selection reverts to None or default value 1-2 seconds after click; price not updating; section header not updating

Title: Edit Configured Product in Cart - Reopen Configurator with Previous Selections
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
User logged in; configurable product already in cart with specific options selected (e.g., Section 1: Option A, Section 2: Option B)
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to cart at {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
2. [ASSERT] Verify configured product is listed with its selected options summary
3. [ACT] Click the 'Edit' button on the configured cart line item
4. [ASSERT] Verify configurator reopens (product configurator page or modal)
5. [ASSERT] Verify all previously selected options are pre-populated: Section 1 shows Option A selected, Section 2 shows Option B selected
6. [ASSERT] Verify the running total reflects the previously-saved configuration price
7. [ACT] Change Section 1 selection from Option A to Option C
8. [ASSERT] Verify running total updates to reflect Option C's price delta
9. [ACT] Click 'Add to Cart' or 'Save' to confirm the updated configuration
10. [NAV] Navigate to cart
11. [ASSERT] Verify cart line shows updated configuration (Option C, new price)
Assertions:
[DOM] 'Edit' button or link visible on configured cart line item
[STATE] Configurator reopens pre-populated with previously saved selections - not blank/default state
[STATE] Section 1 pre-selected to Option A, Section 2 pre-selected to Option B (matching saved config)
[MATH] Running total on reopen matches cart line price from before edit
[MATH] Changing Section 1 to Option C updates running total by the correct price delta
[DOM] After saving: cart line shows Option C selected (not the original Option A)
[FORMAT] Updated cart line price matches new configuration total
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] mutation errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
[NETWORK] no 4xx or 5xx on configurator load or cart update
Failure_Signals: Edit button missing from configured cart line; configurator reopens with default/empty selections instead of saved ones; running total on reopen shows wrong price; price not updating after option change; cart not reflecting updated configuration after save

Title: Edit Configured Product in Cart - Price Reflects New Configuration
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
User logged in; configurable product in cart; original config price known (e.g., $725 = base $500 + Section 1 $100 + Section 2 $125)
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
2. [ASSERT] Note original configured cart line price (e.g., $725)
3. [ACT] Click 'Edit' on the configured cart line
4. [ASSERT] Configurator opens with original selections pre-loaded
5. [ACT] Change Section 2 option to a cheaper alternative (e.g., from $125 to $50 delta)
6. [ASSERT] Verify running total updates: new total = $500 + $100 + $50 = $650
7. [ACT] Save the updated configuration
8. [NAV] Navigate to cart
9. [ASSERT] Verify cart line price changed from $725 to $650
10. [ASSERT] Verify cart subtotal recalculated accordingly
Assertions:
[MATH] Running total in configurator updates immediately when option changed: $650 vs original $725
[FORMAT] Cart line price after save shows $650 (not original $725)
[MATH] Cart subtotal reflects price difference: reduced by $75
[DOM] Cart line option summary updated to reflect new Section 2 selection
[STATE] Quantity unchanged after edit (still 1)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] mutation errors[] is empty on cart update
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
[NETWORK] cart update request returns 200
Failure_Signals: Cart price unchanged after editing configuration; running total not updating in configurator; cart subtotal not recalculating; original price persisting instead of updated price

Title: Unavailable Variant Combinations - Incompatible Options Disabled
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
Product page with size and color variations where some combos are unavailable (e.g., XL in Red is out of stock or not offered)
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to a product with size and color variant selectors
2. [ASSERT] Verify both size and color selectors are visible
3. [ACT] Select size 'XL'
4. [ASSERT] Verify that color options unavailable in XL (e.g., Red) are visually disabled or crossed-out
5. [ASSERT] Verify available colors for XL remain selectable
6. [ACT] Attempt to click a disabled color option (Red)
7. [ASSERT] Verify click is blocked or a tooltip/message explains unavailability
8. [ACT] Select a different size (e.g., M)
9. [ASSERT] Verify Red becomes available again for size M
10. [ASSERT] Verify Add to Cart is only enabled when a valid combination is fully selected
Assertions:
[DOM] Unavailable variant options are visually indicated: greyed out, crossed-out, or with a disabled state
[STATE] Clicking a disabled option does nothing OR shows a tooltip such as 'Not available in XL/Red'
[STATE] Available options for the selected size are clearly interactive and not disabled
[DOM] When size changes, color availability dynamically updates - Red re-enables for M
[STATE] Add to Cart button remains disabled until a fully valid combination is selected
[DOM] No 'Add to Cart' enabled state for an unavailable XL/Red combo
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] mutation errors[] is empty if any availability lookup is made
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
[NETWORK] no 4xx errors on variant availability check
Failure_Signals: Disabled options not visually distinct from available ones; clicking disabled option adds unavailable variant to cart; color availability not updating when size changes; Add to Cart enabled for an unavailable combination
